---
title: "RNA Differential Expression Analysis - Blood RNA from Humans"
author: "Uzochukwu Gospel Ukachukwu"
date: "2026-03-15"
output: html_document
---

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

#### Start ####
#Set Directory for your RNA analysis
```{r}

setwd("C:\\Users\\Documents\\RNA Analysis\\DESeq2_analysis")
getwd()

```

#load libraries
```{r}

if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
  install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("DESeq2")
BiocManager::install("edgeR")
BiocManager::install("qvalue")
BiocManager::install("clusterProfiler")
BiocManager::install("EnhancedVolcano")

library(DESeq2)
library(tidyverse)
library(EnhancedVolcano)

```

# Step 1: prepare/import count data
```{r}

rna_count <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\Documents\\RNA Analysis\\DESeq2_analysis\\rna_count.csv",
                      header = TRUE,  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, row.names = 1)

rna_count <- data.frame(sapply(rna_count, as.numeric), row.names = rownames(rna_count))
rna_count
str_detect(rna_count,"1")

head(rna_count)
str(rna_count)
dim(rna_count)

#If you imported count table without rownames, alternatively you can convert first column of count table to rownames as below;

#rna_count <- rna_count %>% remove_rownames %>% column_to_rownames(var="Sample_ID")

```

#Prepare/import coldata
```{r}

rna_coldata <-read.csv("C:\\Users\\Documents\\RNA Analysis\\DESeq2_analysis\\rna_coldata.csv",
                       header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, row.names = 1)
head(rna_coldata)
dim(rna_coldata)

#If you imported metadata table without rownames, alternatively you can convert first column of metadata table to rownames as below;

#rna_coldata <- rna_coldata %>% remove_rownames %>% column_to_rownames(var="Sample_ID")
```

# Set factor levels for rna_coldata
```{r}

rna_coldata <- rna_coldata[,c("Group", "Batch")]
rna_coldata$Group <- factor(rna_coldata$Group)
rna_coldata$Batch <- factor(rna_coldata$Batch)

```

# make sure rna_count matches rna_col_data, and are all in same order
```{r}

all(colnames(rna_count) %in% rownames(rna_coldata))

#make sure they are in same order
all(colnames(rna_count) == rownames(rna_coldata))
dim(rna_count)

```

# Step 2: Create a table of the sum of counts; will be used to  make a Count-matrix plot
```{r}
res.dir <- "Results"
plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir)
dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T) #create directory 

rna_count_sum <- colSums(rna_count, na.rm = TRUE)
rna_count_sum_df <- as.data.frame(rna_count_sum) #convert to dataframe
rna_count_sum_df<- rna_count_sum_df %>% 
                      rename(Count= rna_count_sum)   #Rename column

rna_count_sum_df <- cbind(rna_count_sum_df, rna_coldata[1]) #Add "Groups" column from rna_coldata.

rna_count_sum_df<- rna_count_sum_df %>% 
                      rename(Groups = Group) #Rename column

colnames(rna_count_sum_df)        #inspect the colnames   

#write.table(rna_count_sum_df, file = "Results/rna_count_sum.csv", row.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

```

# To Plot a Count Matrix, use rna_count_sum table
```{r}

count_sum <- rna_count_sum_df   #reassign to count_sum dataframe 

res.dir <- "Plots"
plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "RNA Count Matrix")
dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)

#make a scatter plot
ggplot(count_sum,aes(x = Groups, y= Count, fill = Groups, color = Groups))+
  geom_point(size = 2)+
  geom_text(aes(label = rownames(count_sum)), vjust = 0.5, size = 2) +
  labs(title = "RNA Count Matrix") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))

#save to path
ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "Count Matrix_point.svg"), width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300)

#make a box plot 
ggplot(count_sum,aes(x=Groups, y=Count, fill = Groups))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  labs(title = "RNA Count Matrix")+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))

#save to path
ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "Count Matrix_boxplot.svg"), width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300)

```

#This code is optional (only for inspection purposes)
```{r}
#Create row sum table
rna_count_row_sum <- rowSums(rna_count)
rna_count_row_sum_df <- as.data.frame(rna_count_row_sum)

write.table(rna_count_row_sum_df, file = "Results/rna_count_row_sum.csv", 
            row.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

mean(rna_count_row_sum_df$rna_count_row_sum, na.rm = TRUE) #check mean of row sums
median(rna_count_row_sum_df$rna_count_row_sum, na.rm = TRUE) #check median of row sums

```

# Step 3: Construct a DESeqdataset object
## Check if there are any NAs in the count table
```{r}
#sum up NAs
sum(is.na(rna_count)) 

# This is optional; you may replace NA with zero if very few
if (any(is.na(rna_count))) {
  rna_count[is.na(rna_count)] <- 0
}

head(rna_count)

#Construct the ddseq object
ddseq <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = rna_count, colData = rna_coldata,
                                design = ~ Group + Batch, ignoreRank = FALSE)
assay_ddseq <- ddseq %>% 
              assay(.)        #Optional(just for inspection)
head(assay_ddseq) 

```

# Remove rows with low gene counts and keep filtered genes
```{r}

keep <- rowSums(counts(ddseq) > 11) >= 10   # >11 in ≥10 samples (half of min group size)
ddseq1 <- ddseq[keep,]

assay_ddseq1 <- ddseq1 %>% 
                assay(.)
str(assay_ddseq)

#write.table(assay_ddseq, file = "Results/assay_ddseq.csv", row.names = TRUE,col.names = TRUE, sep = ",")
    
```

# Set a factor level
```{r}

ddseq1$Group <- relevel(ddseq1$Group, ref = "HP_negative")
ddseq1$Group

```
#NOTE: You should collapse the technical replicates if you have any. 
#Never collapse biological replicates

# Step 4: Run Create DESeq (Note: default p_value = 0.1 )
```{r}

ddseq2 <- DESeq(ddseq1, test ='Wald', sfType = 'poscount')
assay_ddseq2 <- assay(ddseq2)
dim(assay_ddseq2)

```

# Step 5: Compare "Patients vs HP_negative" and make histogarm of p-values
```{r}
# Contrast btwn Patients vs HP-negative control
resPt_N <- results(ddseq2, contrast = c("Group","Patients","HP_negative"),  alpha = 0.1)
summary(resPt_N)
#na.omit(resPt_N$padj) #remove rows containing NA

resPt_N0.1 <- resPt_N[which(resPt_N$padj < 0.1),]
summary(resPt_N0.1)

resPt_N0.05 <- resPt_N[which(resPt_N$padj < 0.05),]
summary(resPt_N0.05)

resPt_N0.01 <- resPt_N[which(resPt_N$padj < 0.01),]        
summary(resPt_N0.01)

#convert results(according to alpha) to a dataframe
resPt_N0.1 <- as.data.frame (resPt_N0.1) #Print dataframe
resPt_N0.1

resPt_N0.05 <- as.data.frame(resPt_N0.05) #Print dataframe
resPt_N0.05

resPt_N0.01 <- as.data.frame(resPt_N0.01) #Print dataframe
resPt_N0.01

#Convert row names to column
resPt_N0.1$Genes <- rownames(resPt_N0.1)
resPt_N0.1 <- resPt_N0.1 %>%
              arrange(padj)

resPt_N0.05$Genes <- rownames(resPt_N0.05)
resPt_N0.05 <- resPt_N0.05 %>%
arrange(padj)

resPt_N0.01$Genes <- rownames(resPt_N0.01)
resPt_N0.01 <- resPt_N0.01 %>%
arrange(padj)

#Create directory to save Histogram
res.dir <- "Plots"
plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "Histogram", "Pt_N")
dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)

#Check the p-value histogram distribution
ggplot(resPt_N,aes(x=pvalue)) +
geom_histogram(color = "brown", fill="white", binwidth=0.01)+
  labs(title = "Histogram of P-value Distribution") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "Pt_N Pvalue distribution.tiff"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 600)

```

# Step 5.1: Explore results for "Patients vs HP_negative"
```{r}

#Create directory to save MA plots
res.dir <- "Plots"
plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "MA Plots", "Pt_N")
dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)

library(ggpubr)

#at padj 0.05
p0.05 <- ggmaplot(resPt_N0.1, main = expression("Patients vs HP_negative"),
         fdr = 0.05,fc =1, size = 0.5,
         palette = c("darkred", "blue", "darkgray"),
         genenames = as.vector(resPt_N0.1$Genes),
         legend = "top", top = 20,
         font.label = c("bold", 9),
         font.legend = "bold",
         font.main = "bold",
         select.top.method = c("fc"),
         ggtheme = ggplot2::theme_minimal()
         )

p0.05 + geom_hline(yintercept = c(1, -1), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray")
ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "Deg_Pt_N0.05_maplot.svg"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 600)

#at padj 0.01
p0.01 <- ggmaplot(resPt_N0.1, main = expression("Patients vs HP_negative"),
         fdr = 0.01, fc= 1, size = 0.5,
         palette = c("darkred", "blue", "darkgray"),
         genenames = as.vector(resPt_N0.1$Genes),
         legend = "top", top = 20,
         font.label = c("bold", 9),
         font.legend = "bold",
         font.main = "bold",
         select.top.method = c("fc"),
         ggtheme = ggplot2::theme_minimal()
)
p0.01 + geom_hline(yintercept = c(1, -1), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray")
ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "Deg_Pt_N0.01_maplot.tiff"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 600)


   
#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.05 and Log2FC <= -0.5 / >=0.5
deg_0.05Pt_N_fc_0.5 <- subset(resPt_N0.1, padj<0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=0.5) %>%
  arrange(desc(log2FoldChange))
write.table(deg_0.05Pt_N_fc_0.5, file = "Results/Pt_N/gdegPt_N_0.05_fc_0.5.csv", row.names = FALSE,col.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.05 and Log2FC <= -1 / >=1
   deg_0.05Pt_N <- subset(resPt_N0.1, padj<0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=1) %>%
     arrange(desc(log2FoldChange))
   write.table(deg_0.05Pt_N, file = "Results/Pt_N/gdegPt_N_0.05.csv", row.names = FALSE,col.names = TRUE, sep = ",")
   
#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.01 and Log2FC <= -1 / >=1
   deg_0.01Pt_N <- subset(resPt_N0.1, padj<0.01 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=1)%>%
     arrange(desc(log2FoldChange))
   write.table(deg_0.01Pt_N, file = "Results/Pt_N/gdegPt_N_0.01.csv", row.names = FALSE, col.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.01 and Log2FC <= -0.5 / >=0.5
   deg_0.01Pt_N_fc_0.5 <- subset(resPt_N0.1, padj<0.01 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=0.5) %>%
     arrange(desc(log2FoldChange))
   write.table(deg_0.01Pt_N_fc_0.5, file = "Results/Pt_N/gdegPt_N_0.01_fc_0.5.csv", row.names = FALSE,col.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.005 and Log2FC <= -1 / >=1
   deg_0.005Pt_N <- subset(resPt_N0.1, padj<0.005 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=1)%>%
     arrange(desc(log2FoldChange))
   write.table(deg_0.005Pt_N, file = "Results/Pt_N/gdegPt_N_0.005.csv", row.names = FALSE, col.names = TRUE, sep = ",")   
   
```

# Step 6: Compare "HP_positive vs HP_negative" and make histogarm of p-values
```{r}

#contrasts btwn "HP_positive vs HP_negative"
resP_N <- results(ddseq2, contrast = c("Group", "HP_positive", "HP_negative"), alpha = 0.1)
summary(resP_N)

resP_N0.1 <- resP_N[which(resP_N$padj < 0.1),]
summary(resP_N0.1)

resP_N0.05 <- resP_N[which(resP_N$padj < 0.05),]
summary(resP_N0.05)

resP_N0.01 <- resP_N[which(resP_N$padj < 0.01),]        
summary(resP_N0.01)

#convert results(according to alpha) to a dataframe
resP_N0.1 <- as.data.frame (resP_N0.1) #Print dataframe
resP_N0.1

resP_N0.05 <- as.data.frame(resP_N0.05) #Print dataframe
resP_N0.05

resP_N0.01 <- as.data.frame(resP_N0.01) #Print dataframe
resP_N0.01

#Convert row names to column
resP_N0.1$Genes <- rownames(resP_N0.1)
#resP_N0.1<- na.omit(resP_N0.1)
resP_N0.1 <- resP_N0.1 %>%
  #filter(padj < 0.1) %>%
  arrange(padj)

resP_N0.05$Genes <- rownames(resP_N0.05)
resP_N0.05 <- resP_N0.05 %>%
  arrange(padj)

resP_N0.01$Genes <- rownames(resP_N0.01)
resP_N0.01 <- resP_N0.01 %>%
  arrange(padj)
#Create directory to save Histogram
res.dir <- "Plots"
plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "Histogram", "P_N")
dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)

#create histogram of p-value
#Check the p-value histogram distribution
ggplot(resP_N,aes(x=pvalue)) +
  geom_histogram(color = "brown", fill="white", binwidth=0.01)+
  labs(title = "Histogram of P-value Distribution") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "P_N Pvalue distribution.tiff"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 600)


## contrasts btwn "HP_positive vs HP_negative" at diff. adj. pvalues
#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.05 and Log2FC <= -0.4 / >=0.4
deg_0.05P_N <- subset(resP_N0.05, padj<0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=0.4)%>%
  arrange(padj)
write.table(deg_0.05P_N, file = "Results/P_N/degP_N_0.05.csv", row.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.01 and Log2FC <= -0.4 / >=0.4
deg_0.01P_N <- subset(resP_N0.01, padj<0.01 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=0.4)%>%
  arrange(padj)

write.table(deg_0.01P_N, file = "Results/P_N/degP_N_0.01.csv", row.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

```

# Step 7: Compare "Patients vs HP_positive" and make histogarm of p-values
```{r}
#contrasts btwn Patients and HP_positive

resPt_P <- results(ddseq2, contrast = c("Group","Patients","HP_positive"),  alpha = 0.1)
summary(resPt_P)

resPt_P0.1 <- resPt_P[which(resPt_P$padj < 0.1),]
summary(resPt_P0.1)

resPt_P0.05 <- resPt_P[which(resPt_P$padj < 0.05),]
summary(resPt_P0.05)

resPt_P0.01 <- resPt_P[which(resPt_P$padj < 0.01),]        
summary(resPt_P0.01)

#convert results(according to alpha) to a dataframe
resPt_P0.1 <- as.data.frame (resPt_P0.1) #Print dataframe
resPt_P0.1

resPt_P0.05 <- as.data.frame(resPt_P0.05) #Print dataframe
resPt_P0.05

resPt_P0.01 <- as.data.frame(resPt_P0.01) #Print dataframe
resPt_P0.01


#Convert row names to column
resPt_P0.1$Genes <- rownames(resPt_P0.1)
resPt_P0.1 <- resPt_P0.1 %>%
  arrange(padj)

resPt_P0.05$Genes <- rownames(resPt_P0.05)
resPt_P0.05 <- resPt_P0.05 %>%
  arrange(padj)

resPt_P0.01$Genes <- rownames(resPt_P0.01)
resPt_P0.01 <- resPt_P0.01 %>%
  arrange(padj)


#Create directory to save Histogram
res.dir <- "Plots"
plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "Histogram", "Pt_P")
dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)

#create histogram of p-value
#Check the p-value histogram distribution
ggplot(resPt_P,aes(x=pvalue)) +
  geom_histogram(color = "brown", fill="white", binwidth=0.01)+
  labs(title = "Histogram of P-value Distribution") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "Pt_P Pvalue distribution.svg"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 600)

```

# Step 7.1 : Explore results for "Patients vs HP_positive"
```{r}

#Create directory to save MA plots
res.dir <- "Plots"
plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "MA Plots", "Pt_P")
dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)


library(ggpubr)
ggmaplot(resPt_P0.1, main = expression("Patients vs HP_positive"),
         fdr = 0.05,fc =1, size = 0.5,
         palette = c("darkred", "blue", "darkgray"),
         genenames = as.vector(resPt_P0.1$Genes),
         legend = "top", top = 20,
         font.label = c("bold", 9),
         font.legend = "bold",
         font.main = "bold",
         select.top.method = c("fc"),
         ggtheme = ggplot2::theme_minimal()
)


ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "Deg_Pt_P0.05_maplot.svg"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 600)


ggmaplot(resPt_P0.1, main = expression("Patients vs HP_positive"),
         fdr = 0.01,fc =1, size = 0.5,
         palette = c("darkred", "blue", "darkgray"),
         genenames = as.vector(resPt_P0.1$Genes),
         legend = "top", top = 20,
         font.label = c("bold", 9),
         font.legend = "bold",
         font.main = "bold",
         select.top.method = c("fc"),
         ggtheme = ggplot2::theme_minimal()
)

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "Deg_Pt_P0.01_maplot.svg"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 600)


##DEG extraction
#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.05 and Log2FC <= -1 / >=1
deg_0.05Pt_P <- subset(resPt_P0.05, padj<0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=1)%>%
  arrange(padj)
write.table(deg_0.05Pt_P, file = "Results/Pt_P/degPt_P_0.05.csv", row.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.05 and Log2FC <= -0.5 / >=0.5
deg_0.05Pt_P_fc_0.5 <- subset(resPt_P0.05, padj<0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=0.5)%>%
  arrange(padj)
write.table(deg_0.05Pt_P_fc_0.5, file = "Results/Pt_P/deg_0.05Pt_P_fc_0.5.csv", row.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.01 and Log2FC <= -1 / >=1
deg_0.01Pt_P <- subset(resPt_P0.01, padj<0.01 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=1)%>%
  arrange(padj)
write.table(deg_0.01Pt_P, file = "Results/Pt_P/degPt_P_0.01.csv", row.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.01 and Log2FC <= -0.5 / >=0.5
deg_0.01Pt_P_fc_0.5 <- subset(resPt_P0.01, padj<0.01 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=0.5)%>%
  arrange(padj)
write.table(deg_0.01Pt_P_fc_0.5, file = "Results/Pt_P/deg_0.01Pt_P_fc_0.5.csv", row.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

#Extract differentially expressed genes with padj < 0.005 and Log2FC <= -1 / >=1
deg_0.005Pt_P <- subset(resPt_P0.05, padj<0.005 & abs(log2FoldChange) >=1)%>%
  arrange(padj)
write.table(deg_0.005Pt_P, file = "Results/Pt_P/degPt_P_0.005.csv", row.names = TRUE, sep = ",")

#### End ####
```

# Step 8: Volcano plots
```{r}

# 8.1: Volcano Plot Patients vs HP-negative

library(ggrepel)
res.dir <- "Plots"
plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "Volcano Plots", "Pt_N")
dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)

diffgen_Pt_N <- resPt_N0.1
diffgen_Pt_N$diffexpressed <- "NO"
diffgen_Pt_N$diffexpressed[diffgen_Pt_N$log2FoldChange > 1 & diffgen_Pt_N$padj < 0.05] <- "UP"
diffgen_Pt_N$diffexpressed[diffgen_Pt_N$log2FoldChange < -1  & diffgen_Pt_N$padj < 0.05] <- "DOWN"

#diffgen_Pt_N$delabel<-NA 

#at padj 0.05
EnhancedVolcano(diffgen_Pt_N, x = "log2FoldChange" ,y = "pvalue" , lab = diffgen_Pt_N$Genes,labSize = 0, 
                pointSize = 2, subtitle = bquote(italic(Gastritis_vs_HP-negative)),pCutoff = 5e-02, 
                FCcutoff = 1, pCutoffCol = "padj",
                xlim = c(-2,3), ylim= c(0,25), ylab = bquote(~-Log[10] ~ italic(Padj)), 
                subtitleLabSize = 18, axisLabSize = 16,
                legendLabels = c("Non Sig. (padj > 0.05)", expression(Log[2] ~ FC), "Sig. (padj < 0.05)", 
                                 expression(Sig. ~ padj ~ and ~ log[2] ~ FC > 1)),
                legendPosition = "right", legendLabSize = 12, legendIconSize = 4, title = NULL)
                

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "deg_0.05Pt_N.tiff"), width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#at padj 0.01
EnhancedVolcano(diffgen_Pt_N, x = "log2FoldChange",y = "pvalue", lab = diffgen_Pt_N$Genes, labSize = 3.5, 
                pointSize = 2, subtitle = bquote(italic(Patients_vs_HP-neg)),pCutoff = 1e-02, FCcutoff = 1,
                pCutoffCol = "padj",
                xlim = c(-2,2), ylim= c(0,25), ylab = bquote(~-Log[10] ~ italic(Padj)), 
            legendLabels = c("Non Sig. (padj > 0.01)", expression(Non ~ Sig. ~ Log[2] ~ FC > 1), 
                             "Sig.  (padj < 0.01)", expression(Sig. ~ padj ~ and ~ log[2] ~ FC > 1)),
            legendPosition = "right", legendLabSize = 9, legendIconSize = 4, title = NULL)

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "deg_0.01Pt_N.svg"), width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#### End ####


# 8.2: Volcano Plot HP-pos vs HP-neg  
res.dir <- "Plots"
plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "Volcano Plots","P_N")
dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)  

diffgen_P_N <- resP_N0.1
diffgen_P_N$diffexpressed <- "NO"
diffgen_P_N$diffexpressed[diffgen_P_N$log2FoldChange > 1 & diffgen_P_N$padj < 0.05] <- "UP"
diffgen_P_N$diffexpressed[diffgen_P_N$log2FoldChange < -1  & diffgen_P_N$padj < 0.05] <- "DOWN"
  
#at padj 0.05
EnhancedVolcano(diffgen_P_N, x = "log2FoldChange",y = "pvalue", lab = diffgen_P_N$Genes, 
              labSize = 0, pointSize = 3, subtitle = bquote(italic("HP-positive_vs_HP-negative")),
              pCutoff = 5e-02, FCcutoff = 0.5, pCutoffCol = "padj", subtitleLabSize = 18, axisLabSize = 16,
              xlim = c(-2,2), ylim= c(0,6), ylab = bquote(~-Log[10] ~ italic(Padj)),
              #legendPosition = "none", title = NULL),
              legendLabels = c("Non Sig. (padj > 0.05)", expression(Non ~ Sig. ~ Log[2] ~ FC > 1), 
                               "Sig.(padj < 0.05, log2FoldChange > 1 )", 
                               expression(Sig. ~ padj ~ and ~ log[2] ~ FC > 1)),
              legendPosition = "right", legendLabSize = 10, legendIconSize = 4, title = NULL)

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "deg_0.05P_N.tiff"), width = 6, height = 7, dpi = 300) 
  
#at padj 0.01
EnhancedVolcano(diffgen_P_N, x = "log2FoldChange",y = "pvalue", lab = diffgen_P_N$Genes, labSize = 3.5, 
                pointSize = 2, subtitle = bquote(italic("HP_pos vs HP-neg")),pCutoff = 1e-02, FCcutoff = 1,
                pCutoffCol = "padj",
                xlim = c(-2,2), ylim= c(0,6), ylab = bquote(~-Log[10] ~ italic(Padj)), 
                legendLabels = c("Non Sig. (padj > 0.01)", expression(Non ~ Sig. ~ Log[2] ~ FC > 1), 
                                 "Sig.  (padj < 0.01)", expression(Sig. ~ padj ~ and ~ log[2] ~ FC > 1)),
                legendPosition = "right", legendLabSize = 9, legendIconSize = 4, title = NULL)

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "deg_0.01P_N.tiff"), width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#### End ####



# 8.3: Volcano Plot Patients vs HP-pos

res.dir <- "Plots"
plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "Volcano Plots", "Pt_P")
dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)

diffgen_Pt_P <- resPt_P0.1
diffgen_Pt_P$diffexpressed <- "NO"
diffgen_Pt_P$diffexpressed[diffgen_Pt_P$log2FoldChange > 1 & diffgen_Pt_P$padj < 0.05] <- "UP"
diffgen_Pt_P$diffexpressed[diffgen_Pt_P$log2FoldChange < -1  & diffgen_Pt_P$padj < 0.05] <- "DOWN"

#at padj 0.05
EnhancedVolcano(diffgen_Pt_P, x = "log2FoldChange",y = "pvalue", lab = diffgen_Pt_P$Genes, labSize = 0, 
                pointSize = 2, subtitle = bquote(italic(Gastritis_vs_HP-positive)),pCutoff = 5e-02, 
                FCcutoff = 1, pCutoffCol = "padj", subtitleLabSize = 18, axisLabSize = 16,
                xlim = c(-2,3), ylim= c(0,22), ylab = bquote(~-Log[10] ~ italic(Padj)), 
                legendLabels = c("Non Sig. (padj > 0.05)", expression(Non ~ Sig. ~ Log[2] ~ FC > 1), 
                                 "Sig. (padj < 0.05)", expression(Sig. ~ padj ~ and ~ log[2] ~ FC > 1)),
                legendPosition = "right", legendLabSize = 12, legendIconSize = 4, title = NULL)

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "deg_0.05Pt_P.tiff"), width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#at padj 0.01
EnhancedVolcano(diffgen_Pt_P, x = "log2FoldChange",y = "padj", lab = diffgen_Pt_P$Genes, labSize = 3, 
                pointSize = 2, subtitle = bquote(italic(Patients_vs_HP-pos)),pCutoff = 1e-02, 
                FCcutoff = 1, pCutoffCol = "padj", subtitleLabSize = 18, axisLabSize = 16,
                xlim = c(-2,2), ylim= c(0,22), ylab = bquote(~-Log[10] ~ italic(Padj)), 
                legendLabels = c("Non Sig. (padj > 0.01)", expression(Non ~ Sig. ~ Log[2] ~ FC > 1),
                                "Sig.  (padj < 0.01)", expression(Sig. ~ padj ~ and ~ log[2] ~ FC > 1)),
                legendPosition = "right", legendLabSize = 10, legendIconSize = 4, title = NULL)

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "deg_0.01Pt_P.svg"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

help("EnhancedVolcano")

#### End ####

```

# Step 9: PCA Plot 
```{r}
res.dir <- "Plots"
  plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "PCA Plots")
  dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)
  
#First estimate the size factors of the ddseq object
ddseq_est <- estimateSizeFactors(ddseq1)

#Use vst() to create transformed values to generate a pca plot, rlog() takes much time to transform the deseq object

vsd <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(ddseq_est)
assay_vsd <- assay(vsd)

## Exclude "Group" levels like "Unclassified" 
ddseq1_subset <- ddseq1[, colData(ddseq1)$Group != "Unclassified"]
colData(ddseq1_subset)$Group <- droplevels(colData(ddseq1_subset)$Group) #drop unused factor levels
  
ddseq_est1 <- estimateSizeFactors(ddseq1_subset) #estimate the size factors of the ddseq object
  
## Use vst() to create transformed values 
vsd1 <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(ddseq_est1)
assay_vsd1 <- assay(vsd1)

```

# Step 10: Normal PCA Plot without Batch Effect Removal
```{r}

  vsd_nbc <- vsd     # vsd for no batch correction

  pca_plot <- plotPCA(vsd_nbc,intgroup=c("Group"), ntop = 1000)
  pca_plot

  ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "PCA-plot-nbc-1000.tiff"), width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 600)
  
  ## without Group Level "Unclassified"
  vsd_nbc1 <-vsd1   # vsd1 for no batch correction
  
  pca_plot1 <- plotPCA(vsd_nbc1,intgroup=c("Group"), ntop = 1000)
  pca_plot1
  
  ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "PCA-plot1-nbc-1000.svg"), width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 600)
  
#### End ####
  

```

# Step 11: Remove batch effect with limma and PCA plot
```{r}

vsd_bc <- vsd    # vsd for batch correction

design.mat <- model.matrix(~ Group, rna_coldata)

assay(vsd_bc) <- limma::removeBatchEffect(assay(vsd_bc),
                                           batch=vsd_bc$Batch,
                                           #batch2=vsd_bc$Date,
                                           design = design.mat)

#class(assay(counts_batch_corrected))
#counts_batch_corrected_df <- as.data.frame(counts_batch_corrected)
   
pca_plot <- plotPCA(vsd_bc,intgroup=c("Group"), ntop = 1000, returnData = FALSE, pcsToUse = 1:2)+
            labs(title = "PCA plot")+
            theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))    
pca_plot
      
ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "PCA-plot_1000.tiff"), width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 600)

## without Group Level "Unclassified"
vsd_bc1 <- vsd1 # assign to another object to avoid losing metatdata
  
rna_coldata_no_unc <- rna_coldata[rna_coldata$Group != "Unclassified", ]
  
design.mat1 <- model.matrix(~ Group, rna_coldata_no_unc)
  
assay(vsd_bc1) <- limma::removeBatchEffect(assay(vsd_bc1),
                                            batch=vsd_bc1$Batch,
                                            #batch2=vsd_bc$Date,
                                            design = design.mat1)
  
assay_vsd_bc1 <- assay(vsd_bc1)
anyNA(assay_vsd_bc1)
  
  
pca_plot1 <- plotPCA(vsd_bc1,intgroup=c("Group"), ntop = 1000, returnData = FALSE, pcsToUse = 1:2)+
            labs(title = "PCA plot")+
            theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))    
pca_plot1
  
ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "PCA-plot1_1000.svg"), width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 600)

```

# Step 12: Plot Dispersion Estimate
```{r}
##plot dispersion estimates. Use Analyzed Deseq Object, "ddseq2" 
  res.dir <- "Plots"
  plot.dir <- file.path(res.dir, "DispEsts")
  dir.create(plot.dir, recursive = T)
   
  pdf(file.path(plot.dir, "DispEsts.pdf"),width = 8, height = 6)
  plotDispEsts(ddseq2)
  dev.off()
  
## without Group Level "Unclassified"
  
  ddseq3 <- DESeq(ddseq1_subset, test ='Wald', sfType = 'poscount') 
  
  pdf(file.path(plot.dir, "DispEsts1.pdf"),width = 8, height = 6)
  plotDispEsts(ddseq3)
  dev.off()

library(reshape2)
  
  mcols <- as.data.frame(mcols(ddseq2))

  DispEsts <- as.data.frame(mcols(ddseq2)) %>% 
             select(baseMean, dispGeneEst,dispersion, dispFit) %>% #rearrange dispFit to appear in front of the chart
             melt(id.vars="baseMean") %>% 
             filter(baseMean>0) %>% 
             ggplot(aes(x=baseMean, y=value, colour=variable)) + 
             geom_point(size=1) +
             scale_x_log10(breaks = c(1, 100, 10000, 1e+06)) + 
             scale_y_log10(breaks = c(100, 1, 0.01, 0.0001, 0.000001)) +
             theme_bw() + 
             ylab("Dispersion") + 
             xlab("BaseMean") +
             scale_colour_manual(
             values=c("Black","#377eb8","#e41a1c"), 
             breaks=c("dispGeneEst","dispersion", "dispFit"), 
             labels=c("Estimate","Final","Fit"),
             name="") +
             guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=2)))

DispEsts

ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "PC_DispEsts.tiff"), width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 600)

## Note that you can replace ddseq2 with ddseq3

```

# Step 13: Diagnose outliers with cooks distance
```{r}

cooks <- log10(assays(ddseq2)[["cooks"]])
cooks_df <- as.data.frame(cooks)
cooks_df$Genes <- rownames(cooks_df)

pivot_cooks <- pivot_longer(cooks_df,-Genes, names_to="Sample_ID",
                            values_to="gene")

  ggplot(pivot_cooks, aes(x=Sample_ID, y=gene)) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = FALSE, staplewidth = 1)+
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-10,0)) +
  ylab("log10 cooks") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 1, hjust = 1, 
                      size = 8, face = "bold")) +
  theme(plot.margin = unit(c(1,1,1,1), "cm", margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 20, l = 10, unit = "pt")), 
        panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill=NA, linewidth=0.5),
        panel.background = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 20, l = 10, unit = "pt")) 

 ggsave(file.path(plot.dir, "Normalised_count.svg"), width = 30, height = 5, dpi = 600)

```
#### End ####
